settings.label: Diretta Target
settings.description: Target index (run 'sudo slim2diretta --list-targets' to find available targets)

settings.label: LMS Server
settings.description: LMS server IP address. Empty = auto-discovery.

settings.label: Player Name
settings.description: Player name shown in LMS. Empty = hostname.

settings.label: Decoder Backend
settings.description: Audio decoder: native (libFLAC) or ffmpeg. FFmpeg may sound different due to its internal processing pipeline.

settings.label: Verbose Logging
settings.description: Enable debug logging output

settings.label: Audio Core(s)
settings.description: Pin SDK worker + Diretta hot path to CPU core(s). Single core (e.g. '3') or comma-separated list (e.g. '3,4'). Empty = no pinning.

settings.label: Other Core(s)
settings.description: Pin audio/decode/slimproto threads to CPU core(s). Single core or comma-separated list. Empty = no pinning.

settings.label: Target Network Interface
settings.description: Interface name (eth0, enp1s0) for the link to the Diretta target. Leave empty to disable link tuning. Requires the ethtool package on the host.

settings.label: Target Speed
settings.description: Forced link speed. WARNING: 100 Mbit may underrun on DSD512+ (~45 Mbps raw) and 10 Mbit is only safe up to ~96 kHz PCM. Stay on 1000 Mbit if you stream hi-res PCM or DSD.

settings.label: Target Duplex Mode
settings.description: Forced duplex mode. Use 'full' unless you have a specific reason to test 'half'.

settings.label: IRQ Affinity Interface(s)
settings.description: NIC name (eth0, enp1s0) whose hardware interrupts will be pinned to specific CPUs. Comma-separated list supported (e.g. 'enp1s0,enp2s0') for setups with one NIC for the upstream LMS source and another for the Diretta target. Matches all related IRQs including MSI-X queues. Leave empty to disable IRQ pinning.

settings.label: IRQ Affinity CPU(s)
settings.description: CPU list for NIC interrupts (e.g. '0-3', '0,2,4', '0-3,8-11'). Pick CPUs OUTSIDE your Audio Core(s) to keep network IRQ activity away from the audio worker. Some kernel-managed MSI-X queues may be read-only — they are reported as 'skipped' in the launcher log, which is normal.

settings.label: SMT (Hyper-Threading)
settings.description: Toggle SMT system-wide at service start. WARNING: affects the whole machine, not just slim2diretta. Topology changes when SMT toggles — your Audio/Other Core(s) values must reference logical CPUs that exist in the chosen state (e.g., a 12-core CPU exposes CPUs 0-23 with SMT on, 0-11 with SMT off). Toggle is non-persistent across kernel reboots; the wrapper re-applies it on every service start. BIOS-level disable takes precedence. Leave on '(no change)' if unsure.

settings.label: PCM Buffer (seconds)
settings.description: PCM ring buffer size in seconds. Empty = default (0.5s).

settings.label: DSD Buffer (seconds)
settings.description: DSD ring buffer size in seconds. Empty = default (0.8s).

settings.label: PCM Prefill (ms)
settings.description: PCM prefill duration before playback starts. Empty = default (80ms).

settings.label: DSD Prefill (ms)
settings.description: DSD prefill duration before playback starts. Empty = default (200ms).

settings.label: Nice Level
settings.description: CPU scheduling priority (-20 = highest, 19 = lowest). Default: -10

settings.label: I/O Scheduling Class
settings.description: I/O priority class for disk and network operations

settings.label: I/O Priority
settings.description: Priority within I/O class (0 = highest, 7 = lowest). Not used with idle class.

settings.label: RT Priority (SCHED_FIFO)
settings.description: Real-time priority for the audio worker thread (1-99). Higher = more priority. Default: 50

settings.label: Thread Mode
settings.description: SDK thread scheduling bitmask: 1=Critical, 2=NoShortSleep, 4=NoSleep4Core, 8=SocketNoBlock, 16=Occupied, 2048=NoSleepForce, 8192=NoJumboFrame

settings.label: Transfer Mode
settings.description: Packet sizing and scheduling strategy

settings.label: Cycle Time (us)
settings.description: Max packet transmission cycle time in microseconds. Empty = auto-calculated.

settings.label: Cycle Min Time (us)
settings.description: Min cycle time in microseconds (random mode only)

settings.label: Info Cycle (us)
settings.description: Info packet cycle time in microseconds (default: 100000 = 100ms)

settings.label: Target Profile Limit (us)
settings.description: 0 = SelfProfile (stable, default), >0 = TargetProfile with auto-adaptation (experimental)

settings.label: MTU Override (bytes)
settings.description: Force specific MTU. Empty = auto-detect. Common values: 1500, 4096, 9000
